entities.name: Sri Trang Gloves (Thailand) Public Company Limited

financial.note: Auditor mandate effective through December 31, 2026; provided an unqualified opinion on the 2025 yearly report.
Awarded 'Best Sustainability-Linked Loan' in April 2026; underscores STGT's pivot toward ESG-linked financing to lower borrowing costs.

supply_chain.note: STA holds 50.61% equity; STGT's GPM is highly sensitive to the transfer price of concentrated latex from STA's midstream processing plants.
Primary demand source for medical-grade exam gloves; 2026 recovery in hospital utilization rates is a key volume catalyst.
Synthetic glove production (NBR) depends on petrochemical inputs; 2026 global oil volatility impacts synthetic glove margins differently than natural latex ones.

competitors.note: Primary Malaysian rival in the high-end Nitrile segment; competitive pricing in the US/EU markets pressures STGT's export margins.
Global capacity leader; intense price competition from Malaysian peers is a persistent risk for STGT's ASP (Average Selling Price).

macro_commodity_factors.logic: The most critical profit driver; 2026 is seeing a gradual USD-denominated ASP recovery toward USD 19.40+ as pandemic-era oversupply finally clears.
Export-heavy model; a weak Baht (trading ~32.50 in April 2026) is vital for translating USD sales into competitive THB revenue.
Glove manufacturing is energy-intensive; the 2026 reduction in Ft rates provides a direct tailwind to the 'Cost of Sales'.
Input cost for natural rubber gloves; 2026 supply growth due to neutral weather patterns (post-La Niña) helps stabilize raw material costs.
